market_summary: Fourth consecutive weekly loss for major indexes — the longest losing streak in over a year. S&P 500 down roughly 7% from January ATH, now 5% below highs. VIX spiked to 26.78 driven by Middle East conflict (Iran war, Strait of Hormuz closure) and a hawkish Fed that raised its inflation forecast to 2.7%. Triple witching amplified volatility. Energy and defensives outperformed; tech and financials lagged.

new_rules: In VIX > 25 environment, reduce position sizes to 3-5% and favor defensive sectors
Geopolitical escalation trades (energy longs) are crowded — take profits quickly on spikes
Fed policy removes the rate-cut safety net — don't assume the 'Fed put' exists right now
Fourth consecutive weekly loss suggests possible oversold bounce incoming — watch for reversal signals
System just launched — prioritize small positions and data collection over alpha generation
regime_forecast: Risk-off likely continues into next week unless Middle East conflict de-escalation. Key data: Construction Spending, PMI, Michigan Sentiment. If PMI misses, could trigger further selloff. If conflict headlines improve, expect sharp relief rally — VIX mean reversion from 26 would lift broad market.
focus_areas: Monitor energy sector for exhaustion signals (crowded long)
Watch for oversold bounces in quality tech names (MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL)
Track VIX — break below 22 would signal regime shift to neutral
Collect more data points before increasing position sizes
